member.name: T:pcpm.Infrastructure.Configuration.ConfigurationLoader
member.summary: Loads and persists from pcpm.json at the workspace root. Uses source-generated JSON for AOT-friendly deserialisation (no reflection at runtime).

member.name: T:pcpm.Infrastructure.Configuration.WorkspaceLocator
member.summary: Default . Walks up from the current directory looking for pcpm.json or pcpm-workspace.yaml. If neither is found, the current directory is used.

member.name: T:pcpm.Infrastructure.Conversion.ConvertService
member.summary: Converts a workspace to or from Central Package Management by reading and writing project files and Directory.Packages.props.

member.name: T:pcpm.Infrastructure.Cpm.CpmFileService
member.summary:
Reads and writes Directory.Packages.props. The XML shape we produce and consume:
<Project> <PropertyGroup> <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally> </PropertyGroup> <ItemGroup> <PackageVersion Include="X" Version="1.0.0" /> ... </ItemGroup> </Project>
We never touch the file outside the <PropertyGroup> for the flag and the <ItemGroup> for <PackageVersion> entries. Any user-added <GlobalPackageReference> items are preserved.

member.name: T:pcpm.Infrastructure.FileSystem.HardlinkCreator
member.summary:
Cross-platform file-link creator.
Windows: NTFS hardlinks via CreateHardLinkW (kernel32). Zero extra disk — source and link share the same inode data. Requires both paths on the same volume, which is the normal case (%LOCALAPPDATA%\pcpm\store and ~/.nuget/packages are both on the system drive).
macOS / Linux: symlinks via File.CreateSymbolicLink (available since .NET 6). Symlinks work across filesystems and are natively understood by MSBuild and NuGet restore.

member.name: T:pcpm.Infrastructure.FileSystem.PhysicalFileSystem
member.summary: Default backed by the real disk via and . Glob patterns support *, ** and ?.

member.name: M:pcpm.Infrastructure.FileSystem.PhysicalFileSystem.EnumerateGlob(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Minimal glob walker. Supports ** for recursive descent (any number of directory segments, including zero — so **/*.csproj matches a Foo.csproj at the root too), * for any chars in a single segment, ? for one char.

member.name: T:pcpm.Infrastructure.Lockfiles.LockfileService
member.summary: Persists to pcpm.lock at the workspace root. Uses a stable JSON shape (camelCase, indented) so the file is diff-friendly in PRs.

member.name: T:pcpm.Infrastructure.NuGet.NuGetFeed
member.summary:
NuGet v3 feed client. Speaks the public https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json protocol:
List versions: GET /v3/registration5-semver1/{lowerId}/index.json
Get metadata: same URL, page through catalog entries
Download .nupkg: GET <packageContent URL> (a CDN URL like https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/<lowerId>/<version>/<id>.<version>.nupkg)

member.name: F:pcpm.Infrastructure.NuGet.NuGetFeed._indexCache
member.summary: Per-instance cache of registration indexes keyed by lower-case package id. Eliminates the duplicate HTTP round-trip between ListVersionsAsync and GetMetadataAsync, and deduplicates concurrent requests for the same package during parallel resolution. Uses in the factory so one caller's cancellation cannot poison the shared entry.

member.name: M:pcpm.Infrastructure.NuGet.NuGetFeed.FetchIndexAsync(pcpm.Core.Models.PackageId)
member.summary: Fetch the registration index for , caching the in-flight so parallel callers for the same package share one HTTP request.

member.name: M:pcpm.Infrastructure.NuGet.NuGetFeed.FetchAndHydrateAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Fetch the registration index and concurrently hydrate any page stubs. NuGet's registration API is paged for packages with many versions: the top-level index returns pages where Items is null and only the @id URL is set. We fetch those pages in parallel so callers always get a fully-populated index.

member.name: T:pcpm.Infrastructure.NuGet.NuGetFeedException
member.summary: Wrapper to surface NuGet-protocol errors as our own exception type.

member.name: T:pcpm.Infrastructure.Process.ProcessRunner
member.summary: Default backed by . Streams stdout/stderr to captured strings; the caller can tee them through Spectre.Console for live output if needed.

member.name: T:pcpm.Infrastructure.Project.ProjectDiscoveryService
member.summary: Default . Reads pcpm-workspace.yaml if it exists and uses its packages patterns; otherwise falls back to **/*.csproj. The YAML parser is intentionally tiny — just enough for the two-line shape we write (no need to pull in YamlDotNet for this).

member.name: T:pcpm.Infrastructure.Project.ProjectFileService
member.summary: Reads and writes <PackageReference> entries in a .csproj file. In CPM mode the project declares the id only (no Version attribute — versions live in Directory.Packages.props). We never touch the project file outside the <ItemGroup> that contains <PackageReference>.

member.name: T:pcpm.Infrastructure.Store.PackageStore
member.summary:
Content-addressable package store, pnpm-style.
Layout under :
v1/ <sha256-hex>/ pkg.nupkg # the original downloaded payload, immutable extracted/ # the unzipped package contents (one-time extract) <id>/<version>/lib/net10.0/X.dll ...
The hardlink to ~/.nuget/packages is created by . The whole point: each unique package version is stored on disk exactly once, and every project that uses it points to the same bytes via NTFS hardlinks. Disk usage stays flat, not O(projects × deps).

member.name: M:pcpm.Infrastructure.Store.PackageStore.DefaultStorePath
member.summary:
Default store path. Resolution order (mirrors pnpm's PNPM_HOME convention):
PCPM_HOME env var (if set) → $PCPM_HOME/store
Windows fallback → %LOCALAPPDATA%/pcpm/store
Unix fallback → $XDG_DATA_HOME/pcpm/store (or ~/.local/share/pcpm/store)

member.name: M:pcpm.Infrastructure.Store.PackageStore.DefaultGlobalPackagesFolder
member.summary: Default global NuGet packages folder (same env var rules as dotnet restore).
